records.ety: [Gr. <?/ the first shoot of the vine, the vine blossom, the vine; o'i`nh the vine + <?/ bloom, 'a`nqos flower.]

defs: Having, or imparting, the odor characteristic of the bouquet of wine; specifically used, formerly, to designate an acid (oenanthic acid) whose ethereal salts were supposed to occasion the peculiar bouquet, or aroma, of old wine. Cf. oenanthylic.
